summary: Matched several gmmain_lib routines and added concrete data definitions for default GameRules and a custom GXRenderModeObj. The PR replaced multiple nonmatching placeholders with C implementations for rules randomization, zeroing substructures, VI render-mode selection, main-lib initialization, and reset-code bookkeeping. It also tightened gmmain_lib.h prototypes and moved pad_F0 into struct gmm_retval_EDBC in types.h, indicating the struct boundary was adjusted to support matching and correct field layout.
what_changed: Added includes needed by newly matched gmmain_lib code: HSD random/video, gm_1601, gm types, lb_00B0, lbaudio_ax, and lblanguage.

Defined gmMainLib_803D4A48 as a default GameRules initializer with positional field comments for known fields such as mode, time limit, stock count, handicap, damage ratio, stock time limit, friendly fire, pause, score display, and unk_14.
Defined gmMainLib_803D4A80 as a GXRenderModeObj using VI_TVMODE_NTSC_PROG, VI_XFBMODE_SF, repeated sample patterns, and a vertical filter table.
Removed the trivial func_8015CFAC stub returning 0.
Implemented gmMainLib_8015ECBC to set gmMainLib_804D3EE0->x1850.unk_x1 to 0x34 normally, or randomly to 0x34/0x36 when gm_80164600() and gm_80164ABC() both hold, using HSD_Randi(4).
Implemented gmMainLib_8015F464, gmMainLib_8015F490, and gmMainLib_8015F4BC as memzero wrappers for gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.unk_8, unk_28, and unk_30 respectively.

Implemented gmMainLib_8015F500 to select between gmMainLib_803D4A80, GXNtsc480Prog, GXNtsc480IntDf, and GXNtsc480Int based on gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x8 and gmMainLib_804D3EE0->thing.x1CB0.x10[5], then call HSD_VISetConfigure.

Implemented gmMainLib_8015FBA4 under #pragma dont_inline: clears gmMainLib_804D3EE0, detects /usa.ini with DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, sets language settings accordingly, seeds gmMainLib_8045A6C0[0].x1850 from gmMainLib_803D4A48, initializes slots 1 through 8 with gmMainLib_8015F600(i, 1), initializes audio with lbAudioAx_80028690, and configures video through gmMainLib_8015F500.
Adjusted gmMainLib_8015FCC0 to write through a temporary pointer to gmMainLib_8046B0F0, preserving reset-code state and lbTime_8000AFBC timestamp assignment.

Fixed gmmain_lib.h declarations: corrected gmMainLib_8015EDC8 address comment, added u8 gmMainLib_8015F4E8(void), and changed gmMainLib_8015F600 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void gmMainLib_8015F600(int, int).
Moved char pad_F0[0xB8] from struct gmm_x1868 into struct gmm_retval_EDBC, making unk_30 include that padding instead of keeping it at the outer gmm_x1868 level.
smart_moves: Kept the large GameRules initializer positional but annotated known fields inline, which preserves MWCC-compatible C while making fragile struct-order data understandable.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gmMainLib_8015FBA4, suggesting inlining control was necessary for matching without globally affecting surrounding codegen.
Introduced a local temporary pointer in gmMainLib_8015FCC0 for gmMainLib_8046B0F0, a small code-shape tactic often needed to match MWCC register/addressing behavior.
Modeled the render-mode choice as pointer selection followed by a single HSD_VISetConfigure call, matching the control flow while centralizing the final side effect.
Adjusted struct padding ownership rather than only forcing casts or raw offsets, improving future type correctness for gmm_retval_EDBC and gmm_x1868 users.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC-era C in this project, designated initializers are not available, so default data structs may need positional initializers plus comments for known fields.
When a large initializer is unavoidable, comments beside fields can document inferred member meanings without changing generated data layout.
Matching can require explicit no-inline pragmas around initialization routines; use scoped push/pop pragmas to avoid accidental codegen changes elsewhere.
If a zeroing function targets an embedded object, prefer expressing it as memzero(&field, sizeof(field)) once the struct layout supports that expression.

A padding relocation inside nested structs can be a real decomp improvement: here pad_F0 became part of gmm_retval_EDBC instead of gmm_x1868, allowing unk_30 sizing and zeroing to make more semantic sense.
Small temporary variables, especially for globals and selected pointers, may be matching tactics rather than style noise.
naming_conventions: Unidentified global data and functions continue to use address-based names such as gmMainLib_803D4A48, gmMainLib_803D4A80, gmMainLib_8015F500, and gmMainLib_8046B0F0.
Game-mode structs use gmm_ prefixes for partially understood types, such as gmm_x1F2C, gmm_x1868, gmm_retval_ED98, gmm_retval_EDB0, and gmm_retval_EDBC.
Unknown or inferred fields retain offset-style names such as x1850, unk_x1, thing, unk_8, unk_28, unk_30, x1CB0, x10, and pad_F0.
Header comments use address-offset markers like /* 15F500 */ and were corrected when stale, as with gmMainLib_8015EDC8 changing from /* 15F4E8 */ to /* 15EDC8 */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline for gmMainLib_8015FBA4 to prevent MWCC from inlining calls and disrupting the expected match.
Kept an unused stack dummy declaration `u8 _[4];` in gmMainLib_8015ECBC, likely to influence stack frame/code shape; the diff does not provide reviewer discussion for this exact tactic.
Split render-mode selection into intermediate pointer variables var_r0, var_r0_2, and var_r3, preserving compiler-like branch and register behavior.
Assigned gmMainLib_8046B0F0 through a local `struct gmMainLib_8046B0F0_t* tmp`, likely to match address materialization and repeated stores.
Replaced placeholders with direct memzero calls using sizeof on inferred fields, reducing raw-size dependence where field layout was known.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether there was a better way to write the initializer comments in C.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Explained that designated initializers would be preferable, but MWCC does not support them, so positional initializers with comments were used.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gmMainLib_803D4A48 or document the GameRules fields used in its positional initializer.
Search for later changes to gmMainLib_8015FBA4 to see whether the dont_inline pragma remained necessary.
Search for additional struct layout refinements involving gmm_retval_EDBC, gmm_x1868, unk_30, and pad_F0.
Look for later matches of gmMainLib_8015F260, gmMainLib_8015F588, gmMainLib_8015F600, gmMainLib_8015FB68, and gmMainLib_8015FC74.
Check whether func_8015CFAC was removed as dead/trivial code or renamed elsewhere in subsequent cleanup.
